question: [File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user cause the health() method to always return UP by triggering any exception in prometheusClient.query(), thereby suppressing detection of a lagging importer that is processing tampered Hashgraph stream data?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the broad catch(Exception e) block in health() to silently mask an importer that has stopped processing Hashgraph records entirely, causing the monitor to report UP while tampered history goes undetected?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: evaluate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause evaluate() to return up() by ensuring the Prometheus response has a null resp value, thereby hiding that the importer has stopped ingesting Hashgraph consensus records and allowing history tampering to go undetected?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: evaluate()] Can an unprivileged external user cause evaluate() to return up() by ensuring series.isEmpty() is true (e.g., by suppressing Prometheus metric emission from the importer), hiding that the importer has fallen behind on Hashgraph stream ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: evaluate()] Can an unprivileged external user manipulate the Prometheus series response so that no entry matches properties.getLocalCluster(), causing lagByCluster.get(localCluster) to return null, making isLagAboveThreshold(null) return false and evaluate() return up() while the local importer is processing tampered Hashgraph data?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: isLagAboveThreshold()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a Prometheus series value of 'NaN' or 'Infinity' for the local cluster so that Double.isFinite(lagSeconds) returns false in isLagAboveThreshold(), causing the health check to return UP even when the importer is severely lagging behind on Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: isLagAboveThreshold()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a Prometheus series value of exactly 0.0 or a negative number for the local cluster lag so that lagSeconds > properties.getThresholdSeconds() is false in isLagAboveThreshold(), masking a real importer lag and allowing Hashgraph history manipulation to go undetected?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: isOtherClearlyBetter()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a fabricated Prometheus series for a competing cluster with a very low (or negative) lag value so that isOtherClearlyBetter() returns false, preventing the health indicator from reporting DOWN even when the local importer is severely lagging on Hashgraph stream data?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: isOtherClearlyBetter()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a Prometheus series for a competing cluster with a lag value of negative infinity so that (bestOther.getAsDouble() + margin) is always less than any localLag, causing isOtherClearlyBetter() to always return true and the health indicator to always report DOWN, creating a denial-of-service that masks the real Hashgraph importer state?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: parseLagByCluster()] Can an unprivileged external user inject duplicate cluster entries in the Prometheus response so that the merge function (_, b) -> b in parseLagByCluster() always picks the last value, allowing an attacker to override the local cluster's real lag with a fabricated low value and hide Hashgraph importer delays?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: parseLagByCluster()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a Prometheus response containing a series entry with a cluster name that exactly matches localCluster but with a fabricated low lag value, causing parseLagByCluster() to map the local cluster to a benign lag and evaluate() to return UP while the real importer is processing tampered Hashgraph records?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: toClusterLagEntry()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a Prometheus series where value.size() is exactly 1 (missing the lag value at index 1) so that toClusterLagEntry() returns Optional.empty(), causing the local cluster to be absent from lagByCluster and evaluate() to return UP while the importer is behind on Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: toClusterLagEntry()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a Prometheus series with a null metric field so that toClusterLagEntry() returns Optional.empty() for the local cluster entry, causing evaluate() to treat localLag as null and return UP while the importer is lagging on Hashgraph stream ingestion?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: toClusterLagEntry()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a Prometheus series with a whitespace-only cluster name so that StringUtils.trimToNull(metric.cluster()) returns null in toClusterLagEntry(), causing the entry to be silently dropped and the local cluster lag to be absent from the health evaluation, hiding Hashgraph importer delays?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: parseDouble()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a Prometheus series value of 'Infinity' as a string so that Double.parseDouble('Infinity') succeeds and returns Double.POSITIVE_INFINITY, which then passes parseDouble() but is filtered by Double.isFinite() in isLagAboveThreshold(), causing the health check to return UP while the importer is severely lagging on Hashgraph data?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: parseDouble()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a Prometheus series value of '-Infinity' for a competing cluster so that bestOtherLag() filters it out via Double::isFinite, making bestOther absent and isOtherClearlyBetter() return false, preventing DOWN status even when the local importer is lagging on Hashgraph stream data?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: parseDouble()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a non-numeric string (e.g., 'tampered') as the lag value in a Prometheus series so that parseDouble() catches NumberFormatException and returns Optional.empty(), silently dropping the local cluster from the lag map and causing evaluate() to return UP while the importer is processing manipulated Hashgraph records?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: buildLagQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user influence the clusterRegex value (via properties.getClusterRegex()) by injecting PromQL metacharacters into a cluster name, causing buildLagQuery() to produce a malformed or semantically altered PromQL query that returns no results, making evaluate() return UP while the importer is lagging on Hashgraph history?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: buildLagQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a cluster name containing PromQL regex special characters (e.g., '.*') into the clusters list so that clusterRegexOrNull() produces a regex that matches all clusters, causing the Prometheus query to return inflated or incorrect lag data and masking the true state of Hashgraph importer processing?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: buildLagQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a cluster name containing the pipe character '|' into the clusters list so that clusterRegexOrNull() produces a regex with unintended alternation, causing the Prometheus query to include or exclude clusters that alter the lag comparison and hide Hashgraph importer delays?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: buildLagQuery()] Can an unprivileged external user inject a cluster name containing a closing brace '}' or quote character into the clusters list so that the formatted PROMETHEUS_LAG_QUERY produces a syntactically broken PromQL query, causing prometheusClient.query() to throw an exception that is caught and returns UP, hiding Hashgraph importer lag?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user cause a network-level timeout on the Prometheus endpoint so that prometheusClient.query() throws an exception, which is caught by the broad catch block in health() and returns UP, masking that the importer has stopped processing Hashgraph consensus stream data?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: health()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that health() returns UP on any exception to perform a denial-of-service against the Prometheus endpoint, ensuring the health indicator never reports DOWN regardless of how far behind the importer is on Hashgraph stream data?

[File: 'File Name: monitor/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/monitor/health/ImporterLagHealthIndicator.java -> Scope: Critical: Any impact caused by Tampering/Manipulating Hashgraph history'] [Function: evaluate()] Can an unprivileged external user craft a Prometheus response where PrometheusQueryResponse.status is not 'success' so that getSeries() returns an empty list, causing evaluate() to return UP and hiding that the importer is lagging behind on Hashgraph history ingestion?
